title: Dense scene-coordinate voting with a differentiable IRLS robust consensus (from-scratch trunk)
hypothesis: 20 straight rounds since champion exp-35 (743.07 m) have failed, and this round's forced direction pre-assigns the ACE/DSAC family: dense per-cell coordinate regression aggregated by a robust consensus, not a global classification field. This exact family (one global descriptor replaced by many local location votes, over the same single-crop-center supervision this project actually has) has been measured twice before, and both measurements point at the AGGREGATOR, not the per-cell voting idea, as the failure point: exp-4 (2563.37 m, reverted) used a plain unweighted mean over per-patch coordinate guesses, and exp-49 (2655.65 m, reverted, ~3.6x worse than champion) used a single learned softmax-attention-weighted mean. Both are one-shot, non-iterative pooling: a single set of weights (uniform, or one attention pass) is computed once and applied once, so an outlier vote that the attention head fails to suppress on its first and only pass drags the final answer proportionally to how much weight it received -- there is no mechanism to notice, after seeing the emerging consensus, that a given vote disagrees with it and correct course. This design tests the one aggregator in this family that has never been measured: iteratively reweighted least squares (IRLS) with a redescending kernel, which recomputes vote weights against the CURRENT consensus estimate over several rounds, so a vote that looked plausible in isolation but disagrees with where the other ~1,023 votes are converging gets progressively down-weighted rather than fixed at its first-pass score. This is mechanically closer to what ACE/DSAC actually do at inference (RANSAC re-scores hypotheses against an evolving consensus) than either prior attempt was, and it is a materially different bet from 'per-patch consensus is refuted' -- it is a bet that per-patch consensus was never fairly tested because both prior attempts used a pooling mechanism with no self-correction.
method: Replace the champion's global-descriptor + 1024-way map-cell classification field entirely with: (1) a from-scratch, no-pretrained conv trunk producing a 16x16 spatial grid of local features (banned identifiers mnv3s_features8.pt / mobilenet_v3_small do not appear anywhere); (2) a per-cell head that independently regresses an absolute (u, v) coordinate guess plus a log-variance (confidence) from each cell's own local feature vector only -- no global pooling anywhere in the forward path; (3) the crop's four 90-degree C4 rotations each get their own independent trunk pass and their own 16x16 grid of votes, pooled together as 4x16x16 = 1,024 total independent votes (previously the four rotations' full field maps were averaged BEFORE any decision; now every rotation's every cell is one independent vote into the consensus stage); (4) a 3-round differentiable IRLS solve: start from a confidence-weighted mean of all 1,024 votes, then for each of 3 rounds recompute each vote's residual distance to the current estimate, apply a fixed-scale Geman-McClure redescending re-weighting (soft-inlier down-weighting of votes far from the emerging consensus), renormalize, and recompute the weighted mean -- fully differentiable, no argmax/hard threshold/data-dependent control flow anywhere, so it is both trainable end-to-end and ONNX-exportable as a fixed 3-iteration unroll; (5) overall confidence derived from the final iteration's effective weight concentration (inverse of the summed squared normalized weights, i.e. how few votes the answer actually rests on) and the final weighted residual spread, fed through a small calibrated head trained the same way as the champion's hit-predictor (BCE against a within-GOOD_ERR_UV label, detached inputs, post-hoc per-bucket conf_shift calibration). The confusability-weighted training sampler (exp-35) is rebuilt at the level of individual training locations (nearest-distant-location descriptor match, same exclusion radius idea) instead of via the now-deleted GRID_K classification grid, since there is no classifier grid left to bucket by. Loss = final-consensus-estimate L2 + 0.5x mean per-vote heteroscedastic NLL (trains each cell's own sigma to track its own actual error) + 0.3x confidence BCE, mirroring the champion's loss-term weighting shape but built for the new decode.
expected_outcome: If a genuinely self-correcting aggregator (IRLS re-scoring against an evolving consensus) is the missing ingredient distinguishing 'dense per-patch consensus done right' from the two prior naive-pooling measurements, worst-case median error should land meaningfully below champion's 743.07 m -- plausibly in the 400-700 m range, since outlier votes get actively suppressed across 3 rounds instead of being scored once and left alone. But the prior art here is two real, clean measurements (not crashes) both landing 3.4-3.6x worse than champion (2563.37 m, 2655.65 m), so a third result in that same range, or worse, is a live possibility and would be a strong, informative result in its own right: it would mean the dense-per-patch-consensus family is refuted at the family level (three distinct aggregators -- plain mean, attention-weighted mean, robust IRLS mean -- all failing against a single-global-descriptor classifier baseline), not merely under-aggregated, and would redirect future rounds firmly away from per-patch/local-vote architectures toward global-descriptor families with a different trunk, loss, or training-data strategy. Mechanism-level signature to check post-hoc: per-cell predicted sigma should correlate negatively with each cell's actual coordinate error (cells that are locally confused about their patch should self-report high uncertainty); the IRLS weight distribution should visibly sharpen from round 1 to round 3 on genuinely well-localized crops (fewer, more concentrated surviving votes) while staying diffuse on genuinely ambiguous crops (a real self-correction signal, not decorative iteration). Falsification forks: (a) metric clears champion with healthy sigma-error correlation and visible weight sharpening -> the aggregator really was the wall, and future rounds should keep exploring this family (more IRLS rounds, learned redescending scale, finer grid); (b) metric lands in the same 2500+ m range as exp-4/exp-49 despite healthy per-round sharpening -> the dense-consensus family itself is now refuted at the family level across three aggregator designs, and future rounds should redirect at global-descriptor trunk/loss/data strategies instead; (c) metric regresses but sigma-error correlation is flat/absent (per-cell confidence never learned to track real error) -> this specific run's heteroscedastic NLL term failed to train properly (a from-scratch-trunk capacity or learning-rate problem, not a refutation of the IRLS mechanism itself), and the next attempt should retry IRLS with a better-tuned NLL weight or a longer schedule before concluding the family is dead.
eli5: Instead of one all-knowing guess about where the photo was taken, we now let roughly a thousand tiny local patches of the same photo each make their own small, independent guess -- like asking a thousand people who each only glimpsed one corner of a map to point at where they think you are. Two earlier attempts at this already failed because they combined all those guesses in one single pass -- either just averaging them all equally, or letting one learned 'attention' pass decide the weights once and stick with it -- so a handful of confused, wrong guesses could still drag the final answer off course. This time the combining step runs several rounds: after each round it checks which guesses agree with the group and which ones look like outliers, and quietly turns down the volume on the outliers before averaging again -- the same trick a surveyor uses when throwing out one wildly-off measurement before re-averaging the rest. If a photo is genuinely confusing (say, featureless forest or open water), the votes never converge and the model honestly says 'not sure' instead of reporting a guess nobody actually agreed on.

stages.name: Camera frame
stages.detail: 128x128 px aerial crop, one of 6 simulated lighting conditions

stages.name: From-scratch spatial trunk
stages.detail: a brand-new, never-pretrained conv stack (three stride-2 stages then one stride-1 refinement, 24->48->64->96 channels) turns the 128x128 photo into a 16x16 grid of local feature vectors -- no borrowed ImageNet weights, no MobileNetV3/ShuffleNetV2 family, no checkpoint file, nothing carried over from any earlier round

stages.name: Rotation-vote pool
stages.detail: the photo's four 90-degree turns each get their own full pass through the trunk, so one crop now produces 4x16x16 = 1,024 independent local glimpses instead of one blended answer -- previously the four turns' full maps were averaged together before any decision was made; now every turn's every cell casts its own separate vote straight into the consensus stage below

stages.name: Per-cell coordinate voters
stages.detail: each of the 1,024 local glimpses independently guesses its own answer to 'where on the map is this crop': its own (map-x, map-y) coordinate guess, plus how sure IT personally is of that guess -- the DSAC/ACE idea of many small local opinions instead of one global answer

stages.name: Robust consensus solve
stages.detail: the 1,024 guesses are combined by repeated re-weighting: start from a confidence-weighted average of all of them, then run three rounds where any guess sitting far from the emerging group answer gets automatically turned down and the average is recomputed -- the same trick a surveyor uses to throw out one wildly-off reading before re-averaging the rest, done in a way the network can still learn through end to end. A featureless, ambiguous glimpse (plain rooftop, open water, forest canopy) ends up contributing almost nothing to the final answer by the third round

stages.name: Confidence
stages.detail: reads how tightly the surviving votes clustered by the end of the re-weighting (a handful of strongly agreeing votes vs. 1,024 guesses still shrugging in every direction) plus how far apart they still are -- when nothing agrees, the overall answer is marked low-confidence and the model would rather say 'no fix' than report a number nobody actually agreed on

stages.name: Training data
stages.detail: the confusability oversampling idea (exp-35) is kept but rebuilt at the level of individual training locations instead of the old 1,024-cell classifier grid, which no longer exists in this design: each location's own small patch of reference imagery is compared directly against other locations more than a few cells away, and look-alike locations are drawn more often during training -- same idea as before, just computed without a classifier grid to bucket by

stages.name: Training signal
stages.detail: each of the 1,024 per-glimpse guesses is graded on how far off it is AND how confidently it claimed to be right (wrong-and-overconfident is penalized harder than wrong-and-uncertain) -- and the final, re-weighted consensus answer is also graded directly, so the whole three-round re-weighting chain gets a real training gradient, not just the individual glimpses on their own

stages.name: Training schedule
stages.detail: one shared learning-rate glide for the whole network -- with nothing pretrained left to protect, there is no separate slow-trunk / fast-head split anymore, same as the last several from-scratch rounds

implementation_brief:
Rewrite model/model.py and model/train.py. FROZEN CONTRACTS TO PRESERVE EXACTLY: (1) train.py's CLI (`python -m model.train --area <area> --out-dir <dir> [--epochs N] [--data-dir D] [--max-crops-per-bucket N] [--seed N]`, same argparse flags, same train_info.json append-log behavior); (2) model.py's export_onnx() must still produce a model taking a 1x3x128x128 float32 input in [0,1] (named 'frame') and returning one output named 'uvc' shaped [[u, v, conf]] with u, v normalized map coords in [0,1] and conf in [0,1] -- pipeline/score.py's predict() calls sess.run(None, {name: x})[0][0] and unpacks exactly 3 floats, do not change this. (3) estimate_position()'s signature and behavior (frame ndarray -> (lat, lon, conf) via px_to_lonlat) is unchanged.

model/model.py -- DELETE: PRETRAINED_TRUNK_PATH, IMAGENET_MEAN/STD, _build_pretrained_trunk(), GRID_K, TARGET_SIGMA_CELLS, DECODE_BETA, _grid_centers(), the entire TinyLocNet class (features/layout_squeeze/loc_logits/dark_logits/gate blend), and loss_fn's CE-against-Gaussian-bump term. No import of torchvision.models.mobilenet_v3_small or any other torchvision/timm backbone constructor may remain anywhere in model/ (backbonecheck.py scans verbatim, including docstrings -- do not even mention mnv3s_features8.pt or mobilenet_v3_small in prose).

BUILD instead: a from-scratch nn.Module trunk, 4 conv blocks (stride 2, 2, 2, 1), channel progression roughly 24->48->64->96, each block Conv2d+BatchNorm2d(or GroupNorm)+ReLU, taking 128x128x3 in and producing a 16x16x96 feature grid (no global average pool anywhere in the forward path -- the whole point is to keep the spatial grid). Keep _c4_stack() (the existing rotation helper, unchanged mechanism, just reuse it) to produce [4N,3,128,128] from [N,3,128,128], run the trunk once on the stacked batch, reshape the [4N,96,16,16] output back to expose 4 separate rotation copies per original crop. A per-cell head is a 1x1 Conv2d(96 -> 3) applied to the full 16x16 grid for every rotation copy, producing per-cell (u, v, log_sigma) at every one of the 4x16x16=1024 (rotation, cell) locations per input crop; clamp log_sigma to e.g. [-4, 4] before exponentiating to sigma for stability. Flatten all 1024 votes per crop into tensors of shape [N, 1024, 2] (coords) and [N, 1024] (sigma).

IRLS consensus (pure tensor ops, no python-level data-dependent branching, so it exports cleanly): w0 = 1/(sigma^2 + eps), normalize w0 to sum to 1 over the 1024 votes (this is the initial confidence-weighted mean's weight vector). est = weighted_sum(votes, w0). Then repeat exactly 3 times: resid2 = squared distance of each vote to est; w = w0 * TAU^2 / (TAU^2 + resid2) (Geman-McClure redescending kernel; TAU is a fixed module-level constant, e.g. TAU_UV = 0.08, tune only if training is unstable -- do not make it a learned parameter this round, keep the new mechanism's moving parts minimal given the project's history of catastrophic failures from added learnable knobs); renormalize w to sum to 1 over the 1024 votes; est = weighted_sum(votes, w). After the 3rd iteration, `est` (shape [N,2]) is the predicted (u,v). Also compute, from the FINAL iteration's w: ess_inv = 1/(sum(w**2)) (effective-sample-size-like concentration statistic -- low means concentrated on few votes), and weighted_resid_spread = sum(w * resid2) at the final iteration (both detached from the coordinate-loss graph the same way the champion's conf_head inputs are detached, so confidence training doesn't perturb the location estimate).

Confidence head: small MLP (e.g. Linear(2,16)+ReLU+Linear(16,1)) over [ess_inv, weighted_resid_spread] (both detached, both should probably be log-scaled or normalized before the MLP for numeric stability -- e.g. log(ess_inv), log(weighted_resid_spread + eps)), sigmoid output, minus a registered conf_shift buffer exactly like the champion's mechanism -- keep conf_shift and its calibration exactly as-is conceptually (train.py's calibrate_conf_shift function can be reused essentially unchanged, since it operates on model(...)[:, 2] output values regardless of what produces them).

forward(x, return_logits=False) should return out=[N,3] (u,v,conf) always, and when return_logits=True additionally return the full [N,1024,2] per-vote coordinate tensor and [N,1024] per-vote sigma tensor (renamed from 'logits' since there is no classification logit anymore -- loss_fn's signature changes accordingly, update the one call site in train.py's training loop: `out, votes, sigmas = model(xb, return_logits=True)` then `loss = loss_fn(out, votes, sigmas, yb)`).

loss_fn(pred, votes, sigmas, target_uv): resid2_per_vote = squared L2 distance from each of the 1024 votes to target_uv (broadcast target to every vote, since every vote is trying to predict the same single crop-center coordinate); nll = mean over all votes and batch of (resid2_per_vote / (2*sigma^2) + log(sigma)); coord_loss = mean squared L2 error of pred[:, :2] (the final IRLS estimate) against target_uv; good = (pred[:,:2] error < GOOD_ERR_UV) exactly as before; conf_loss = BCE(pred[:,2], good) exactly as before. Return coord_loss + 0.5*nll + 0.3*conf_loss. Keep GOOD_ERR_UV as a module constant (unchanged value, 0.05).

model/train.py changes: (1) compute_cell_weights(area, data_dir, meta) -- rewrite to operate per individual training LOCATION instead of per GRID_K grid cell: for each crop location in `crops` (the list from list_crops), compute a small local descriptor (e.g. mean RGB + luminance std over a small patch of reference.tif centered at (cx,cy), same statistic shape as before), then for each location find its nearest OTHER location's descriptor distance excluding locations within some small pixel/index exclusion radius (reuse the spirit of NEIGHBOR_EXCLUDE_R, adapted to raw crop coordinates rather than grid indices -- e.g. exclude any other listed crop within a fixed pixel radius of this one), weight = 1/(nearest_distance + eps), normalize to mean 1.0 over all crops in that area. This returns a length-len(crops) array directly usable as `crop_probs`-style weights (train_area's crop_probs computation, which currently maps GRID_K cells to a per-crop weight via `crop_cell`, simplifies to just use this array directly -- delete the crop_cell/cell_w indexing indirection since weights are now already per-crop). (2) Remove the trunk_params/head_params LR-split entirely (`from model.model import ... build_model ...`, one single param group, single LR e.g. 1e-3, since there is no pretrained trunk to protect) -- one `opt = torch.optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=1e-3)` and keep the existing CosineAnnealingLR schedule as-is. (3) Update `from model.model import GRID_K, build_model, export_onnx, loss_fn` to drop GRID_K (no longer exists) -- `from model.model import build_model, export_onnx, loss_fn`. (4) The `info` dict's `init` field should read `"from-scratch (no pretrained trunk)"` and drop `cell_weight_ratio_p90_p10` (no more grid cells) in favor of an analogous per-location weight-ratio stat computed directly from the new compute_cell_weights array (e.g. `float(np.quantile(loc_w, 0.9) / np.quantile(loc_w, 0.1))`) so the SQLite log still records a confusability-spread signal. calibrate_conf_shift() needs no changes -- it only reads `model(xb)[:, 2]`, which is unaffected by the decode rewrite.
